message:
🌱 Add k3s: Missing network metrics for containers mission (#941)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,81 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "k3s-5891-missing-network-metrics-for-containers",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "k3s: Missing network metrics for containers",
+    "description": "Missing network metrics for containers. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current k3s deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your k3s version and configuration:
```bash
k3s --version
kubectl get nodes
```
This feature requires a working k3s installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review k3s configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant k3s configuration:
```bash
k3s --version
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
```
K3s Version: 1.24.3+k3s1

Node(s) CPU architecture, OS, and Version: 
Linux 5.18.10-200.fc36.aarch64 aarch64 GNU/Linux
Linux 5.18.10-200.fc36.x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

Cluster Configuration: 3 servers, 1 agent

I am missing some metrics in grafana,"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Missing network metrics for containers",
+        "description": "Hmm. The metrics output you shared seems to show that the metrics are present and non-zero. Is there something in particular that's missing? Have you confirmed that you're scraping those metrics? Are you sure the problem is not your prometheus query?
```yaml
---
# https://rancher.com/docs/k3s/latest/en/installation/install-options/server-config/
# https://github.com/PyratLabs/ansible-role-k3s

# (bool) Specify if a host (or host group) are part of the control plane
k3s_control_node: true

# (dict) k3s settings for all control-plane nodes
k3s_server:
  node-ip: "{{ ansible_host }}"
  tls-san:
    # kube-vip
    - "{{ kubevip_address }}"
  # Disable Docker - this will use the default containerd CRI
  docker: false
  node-taint:
    - "node-role.kubernetes.io/master=true:NoSchedule"
  flannel-backend: "none" # This needs to be in quotes
  disable:
  
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
kubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Missing network metrics for containers" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Hmm. The metrics output you shared seems to show that the metrics are present and non-zero. Is there something in particular that's missing? Have you confirmed that you're scraping those metrics? Are you sure the problem is not your prometheus query?",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "---
# https://rancher.com/docs/k3s/latest/en/installation/install-options/server-config/
# https://github.com/PyratLabs/ansible-role-k3s

# (bool) Specify if a host (or host group) are part of the control plane
k3s_control_node: true

# (dict) k3s settings for all control-plane nodes
k3s_server:
  node-ip: "{{ ansible_host }}"
  tls-san:
    # kube-vip
    - "{{ kubevip_address }}"
  # Disable Docker - this will use the default containerd CRI
  docker: false
  node-taint:
    - "node-role.kubernetes.io/master=true:NoSchedule"
  flannel-backend: "none" # This needs to be in quotes
  disable:
    # Disable flannel - replaced with Calico
    - flannel
    # Disable traefik - replaced with ingress-nginx
    - traefik
    # Disable servicelb - replaced with metallb and install with Flux
    - s",
+        "---
# https://rancher.com/docs/k3s/latest/en/installation/install-options/agent-config/
# https://github.com/PyratLabs/ansible-role-k3s

# (bool) Specify if a host (or host group) are part of the control plane
k3s_control_node: false

# (dict) k3s settings for all worker nodes
k3s_agent:
  node-ip: "{{ ansible_host }}"
  kubelet-arg:
    # Allow k8s services to contain TCP and UDP on the same port
    - "feature-gates=MixedProtocolLBService=true"
    # Fix for metrics
    # https://github.com/k3s-io/k3s/issues/473
    - "containerd=/run/k3s/containerd/containerd.sock"",
+        "# Drop cgroup metrics with no container.
      - sourceLabels: [id, container]
        action: drop
        regex: '.+;'"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "k3s",
+      "sandbox",
+      "orchestration",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "k3s"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Pod",
+      "Ingress",
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/k3s-io/k3s/issues/5891",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/k3s-io/k3s"
+    },
+    "reactions": 1,
+    "comments": 12,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "k3s",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running k3s cluster or a system where k3s can be installed."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-13T06:37:38.238Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
